---
title: "Input Files"
---

This page records input filenames, fields, units, and ordering rules.

## File selection

For pose and IMU, the app chooses between two kinds of input data in `scenarios`:

- bundled simulated data: `scene_pose.json` and `scene_ms_imu.json`
- nuScenes data: exactly one matching `scene-XXXX_pose.json` and `scene-XXXX_ms_imu.json` pair. For the app to use nuScenes data, you must do your own nuScenes download. The files from the download are in `nuscenes/can_bus/`.[@nuscenes-canbus-readme; @nuscenes-canbus-api; @nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]

Selection follows this rule:

- if exactly one matching nuScenes pair is present, the app uses that pair
- otherwise, the bundled simulated pair supplies the default run

## Shared rules

- each input file is a JSON array [@nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]
- each entry has integer microsecond `utime` [@nuscenes-canbus-readme]
- entries are in strictly increasing time order [@nuscenes-canbus-readme; @nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]
- quaternion arrays use `[w, x, y, z]` [@nuscenes-schema; @nuimages-schema]
- units are SI, with any page-specific exceptions stated explicitly

## `scene_ms_imu.json`

Role: IMU stream used for initialization and propagation.

Minimal example:

```json
[
  {
    "utime": 1700000000000000,
    "linear_accel": [1.543318572672857, -0.03916157510402117, 9.804608745997307],
    "rotation_rate": [-0.0018336573502681383, -0.0014580203316534287, -0.001036707959881021],
    "q": [0.9568021423210139, 0.0, 0.0, 0.29073985012364273]
  }
]
```

Fields:

- `utime`: integer timestamp in microseconds
- `linear_accel`: specific force in frame `I`, in $\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^2$ [@nuscenes-canbus-readme; @nuscenes-canbus-linear-accel-specific-force-local-2026-03-26]
- `rotation_rate`: angular velocity in frame `I`, in $\mathrm{rad}/\mathrm{s}$ [@nuscenes-canbus-readme]
- `q`: orientation field interpreted in this project as ${}^{A}q_{\fsub{I}}$, mapping IMU coordinates into a gravity-aligned frame with arbitrary yaw [@nuscenes-canbus-readme]

Notes:

- `linear_accel` has length 3 [@nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]
- `rotation_rate` has length 3 [@nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]
- `q` has length 4 [@nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]

## `scene_pose.json`

Role: reference pose stream used for truth lookup, checks, or evaluation. [@nuscenes-canbus-readme; @caesar2020nuscenes; @nuscenes-issue-1111]

Minimal example:

```json
[
  {
    "utime": 1700000000000000,
    "pos": [120.0, -45.0, 0.0],
    "orientation": [0.9939560979566968, 0.0, 0.0, 0.10977830083717481],
    "vel": [8.272592839624389, 0.0, 0.0],
    "accel": [1.4902411831299034, 6.58109833210669e-14, 9.8],
    "rotation_rate": [0.0, 0.0, 7.95530308295156e-15]
  }
]
```

Fields:

- `utime`: integer timestamp in microseconds
- `pos`: global-frame position in meters [@nuscenes-canbus-readme]
- `orientation`: quaternion in `[w, x, y, z]` order [@nuscenes-schema; @nuimages-schema]
- `vel`: ego-frame velocity in $\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$ [@nuscenes-canbus-readme]
- `accel`: ego-frame acceleration in $\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^2$ [@nuscenes-canbus-readme]
- `rotation_rate`: ego-frame angular velocity in $\mathrm{rad}/\mathrm{s}$ [@nuscenes-canbus-readme]

Notes:

- this file provides reference data for truth lookup, checks, and evaluation [@nuscenes-canbus-readme; @caesar2020nuscenes; @nuscenes-issue-1111]
- `pos` has length 3 [@nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]
- `orientation` has length 4 [@nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]
- `vel`, `accel`, and `rotation_rate` each have length 3 [@nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]
- in the dataset shape used here, the pose stream is effectively `pos=[x,y,0]`, `orientation=[w,0,0,z]`, and `vel=[forward,0,0]` [@nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]

## Timing and cadence

- the streams share one time axis
- there must be at least one IMU sample with `utime >= first_gps_utime`
- the pose stream is nominally `50 Hz` and the IMU stream is nominally `100 Hz` [@nuscenes-canbus-readme]
- the timestamps are real microsecond timestamps, so small jitter and occasional larger gaps should be expected [@nuscenes-canbus-local-2026-03-26]
- when GPS-like measurements are generated from pose, their timestamps should line up with pose timestamps

See also:

- [Theory / Conventions](../../theory/conventions/)
- [Theory / Supported Input](../../theory/supported-input/)
